item.title: How Medicaid Can Support Lifeline Eligibility
item.description: Medicaid participation can establish a Lifeline eligibility pathway, but the applicant must still complete official verification and provider enrollment.

item.title: Is an EBT Card Enough to Prove SNAP Participation?
item.description: An EBT card may not show the details needed for Lifeline review; an official benefit or approval document is usually stronger evidence.

item.title: Common National Verifier Mistakes and How to Fix Them
item.description: Most preventable delays involve name, address, date, document quality, duplicate household records, or unfinished follow-up tasks.

item.title: What to Do When No Tablet Provider Is Available
item.description: A missing tablet offer does not mean Lifeline eligibility failed; service and device promotions are separate decisions.

item.title: Lifeline Service Benefits vs Device Promotions
item.description: The federal benefit supports eligible phone or internet service, while a device is governed by a provider’s current promotion and terms.

item.title: How to Compare Lifeline Providers Safely
item.description: A safe comparison starts with official availability and coverage, then examines plan allowances, support, fees, and device conditions.

item.title: Documents That May Help Prevent Lifeline Delays
item.description: A current document that clearly connects the applicant to the qualifying fact is more useful than a card, screenshot, or partial page.

item.title: What One Lifeline Benefit per Household Means
item.description: The rule focuses on people who live together and share income and expenses, so unrelated roommates may be separate households.

item.title: How Lifeline Recertification Works
item.description: Recertification confirms continued eligibility and may be automatic or require a response to an official notice.

item.title: Warning Signs of a Fake Tablet Application Website
item.description: Guarantees, countdowns, unrelated redirects, insecure document requests, hidden fees, and invented endorsements are major warning signs.
